name: Akkermansia pilot showed safety and pasteurized-arm metabolic biomarker movement
summary: The first human Akkermansia pilot provides safety and biomarker support, but the stronger efficacy signal came from the pasteurized arm.
statement: Daily oral supplementation with 10 billion live or pasteurized Akkermansia muciniphila bacteria for three months was safe and well tolerated in overweight or obese insulin-resistant volunteers, and the pasteurized arm improved several metabolic and inflammation-linked biomarkers compared with placebo.
population_or_model: Overweight or obese insulin-resistant adult volunteers
quantitative_note: The trial enrolled 40 participants and 32 completed it; pasteurized A. muciniphila improved insulin sensitivity by 28.62 +/- 7.02%, reduced insulinemia by 34.08 +/- 7.12%, and reduced total cholesterol by 8.68 +/- 2.38% versus placebo.
caveats: The sample was small and exploratory.
The strongest reported biomarker effects were in the pasteurized arm, so the result should not be treated as a pure live-biotherapeutic signal.
Metabolic biomarkers in insulin-resistant adults do not establish aging-function benefit.
Several authors reported patents and company involvement related to Akkermansia.
